title: Parts of complex numbers
description: Determine the real and the imaginary parts of complex numbers

assessment_items.question_content:
$z=6-3i$

**$\text{Re}(z)=$** [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

**$\text{Im}(z)=$**[[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
###Background 

Complex numbers are numbers of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $i$ is the imaginary unit and $\maroonD{a}$ and $\tealD{b}$ are real numbers. [[☃ explanation 1]]

- The real part of $z$ is denoted by $\text{Re}(z)=\maroonD{a}$.

- The imaginary part of $z$ is denoted by $\text{Im}(z)=\tealD{b}.$

###Finding the Real and Imaginary Parts of $z$ 

In this case, $z=\maroonD{6}-\tealD{3}i$ is of the form $\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{6}$ and $\tealD{b}=\tealD{-3}$. Therefore: 

- $\text{Re}(z)=\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{6}$.

- $\text{Im}(z)=\tealD{b}=\tealD{-3}$.

###Summary

- $\text{Re}(z)=\maroonD{6}$.

- $\text{Im}(z)=\tealD{-3}$.

- $\text{Im}(z)=\tealD{b}=\tealD{3.14}$.

###Summary

- $\text{Re}(z)=\maroonD{-19}$ and $\text{Im}(z)=\tealD{3.14}$.
